metadata.title: Kubernetes DISA STIG Checklist (RKE2)
metadata.description: This checklist documents the SRE platform compliance with the DISA STIG for Kubernetes, applied to the RKE2 distribution. RKE2 ships with a CIS hardened profile enabled by default and is the only Kubernetes distribution with a published DISA STIG. Additional hardening is applied via Kyverno policies, Istio service mesh, and the rke2-hardening Ansible role.

summary.automation-notes: RKE2 CIS hardened profile addresses the majority of findings by default. Additional findings are addressed by Kyverno policies, Istio service mesh, and the rke2-hardening Ansible role. Items marked not-reviewed require manual verification in the deployed environment.

findings.title: The Kubernetes Controller Manager must use TLS 1.2, at a minimum, to protect the confidentiality of sensitive data during electronic dissemination
findings.implementation: RKE2 compiles all control plane components with GoBoring Go compiler, enforcing TLS 1.2+ with FIPS-compliant cipher suites by default. The controller manager uses TLS 1.2 minimum for all communications.
findings.check-command: kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l component=kube-controller-manager -o jsonpath='{.items[0].spec.containers[0].command}' | grep tls-min-version

findings.title: The Kubernetes Scheduler must use TLS 1.2, at a minimum
findings.implementation: RKE2 scheduler uses TLS 1.2+ for all communications by default via FIPS-compliant Go crypto modules.
findings.check-command: kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l component=kube-scheduler -o jsonpath='{.items[0].spec.containers[0].command}' | grep tls-min-version

findings.title: The Kubernetes API Server must use TLS 1.2, at a minimum
findings.implementation: RKE2 API server configured with TLS 1.2 minimum via FIPS profile. The --tls-min-version=VersionTLS12 flag is set by default in the CIS hardened profile.
findings.check-command: kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l component=kube-apiserver -o jsonpath='{.items[0].spec.containers[0].command}' | grep tls-min-version

findings.title: The Kubernetes etcd must use TLS to protect the confidentiality of sensitive data during electronic dissemination
findings.implementation: RKE2 embedded etcd uses TLS for all client and peer communication by default. Certificate files are auto-generated and managed by RKE2.

findings.title: The Kubernetes etcd must use TLS 1.2, at a minimum
findings.implementation: RKE2 embedded etcd uses TLS 1.2+ with FIPS-compliant cipher suites.

findings.title: The Kubernetes etcd must enable client authentication to secure service
findings.implementation: RKE2 embedded etcd uses mTLS for client authentication by default. The API server connects to etcd using client certificates generated during cluster bootstrap.
findings.check-command: ps aux | grep etcd | grep client-cert-auth

findings.title: The Kubernetes API Server must enable Node restriction as the admission control plugin
findings.implementation: RKE2 enables the NodeRestriction admission plugin by default in its CIS hardened profile, restricting kubelet API calls to their own node resources.
findings.check-command: kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l component=kube-apiserver -o jsonpath='{.items[0].spec.containers[0].command}' | grep NodeRestriction

findings.title: User-facing components must provide a warning banner when TLS-protected
findings.implementation: Istio ingress gateway provides TLS termination for all user-facing traffic. cert-manager manages TLS certificates. Keycloak login page displays configurable warning banners. SSH banners configured via Ansible.
findings.check-command: kubectl get gateway -n istio-system -o yaml | grep tls

findings.title: The Kubernetes API Server must prohibit communication using TLS version 1.0 and 1.1, and SSL 2.0 and 3.0
findings.implementation: RKE2 API server sets --tls-min-version=VersionTLS12 by default. FIPS mode further restricts to FIPS-approved cipher suites only.
findings.check-command: kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l component=kube-apiserver -o jsonpath='{.items[0].spec.containers[0].command}' | grep tls-min-version

findings.title: The Kubernetes API Server must have anonymous authentication disabled
findings.implementation: RKE2 disables anonymous authentication by default in its CIS hardened profile (--anonymous-auth=false).
findings.check-command: kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l component=kube-apiserver -o jsonpath='{.items[0].spec.containers[0].command}' | grep anonymous-auth

findings.title: The Kubernetes cluster must use non-privileged host ports for user pods
findings.implementation: Kyverno ClusterPolicy restricts host port usage. All services use ClusterIP or NodePort (on approved ports). Istio gateway handles external traffic on standard ports.

findings.title: The Kubernetes cluster must have a pod security policy set
findings.implementation: Pod Security Standards are enforced via Kyverno ClusterPolicies (disallow-privileged, require-run-as-nonroot, require-drop-all-capabilities, disallow-privilege-escalation, restrict-volume-types). Kyverno replaces the deprecated PodSecurityPolicy with Kubernetes-native admission control.

findings.title: The Kubernetes cluster must not accept expired or revoked certificates
findings.implementation: cert-manager automates certificate rotation before expiry. RKE2 control plane certificates are automatically rotated. Istio workload certificates have configurable TTL with automatic rotation.

findings.title: The Kubernetes API Server must have an audit policy set
findings.implementation: RKE2 enables audit logging by default with a comprehensive audit policy. Audit logs capture RequestReceived, ResponseStarted, ResponseComplete, and Panic stages for all API operations.

findings.title: The Kubernetes API Server must have an audit log path set
findings.implementation: RKE2 configures the audit log path by default. Audit logs are written to /var/lib/rancher/rke2/server/logs/audit.log and collected by Alloy for forwarding to Loki.

findings.title: The Kubernetes API Server audit logs must be enabled
findings.implementation: RKE2 enables API server audit logging by default. Logs are collected by Alloy DaemonSet and forwarded to Loki for centralized storage and querying.
findings.check-command: kubectl logs -n kube-system $(kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l component=kube-apiserver -o name | head -1) | head -5

findings.title: The Kubernetes API Server must generate audit records that identify what type of event has occurred
findings.implementation: RKE2 audit policy captures the verb (create, update, delete, get, list, watch, patch) for every API request, identifying the type of event.

findings.title: The Kubernetes API Server must generate audit records when successful/unsuccessful attempts to access privileges occur
findings.implementation: RKE2 audit policy captures both successful and unsuccessful API requests with response status codes, including authorization decisions.

findings.title: The Kubernetes API Server must generate audit records containing the full URI
findings.implementation: RKE2 audit records include the full request URI identifying the target resource.

findings.title: The Kubernetes API Server must have anonymous authentication disabled
findings.implementation: RKE2 disables anonymous authentication by default in its CIS hardened profile. The --anonymous-auth=false flag is set on the API server.
findings.check-command: ps aux | grep kube-apiserver | grep anonymous-auth

findings.title: The Kubernetes API Server must enable RBAC as the authorization mode
findings.implementation: RKE2 enables RBAC (Role-Based Access Control) by default. The --authorization-mode includes RBAC and Node. Tenant namespaces have scoped Roles and RoleBindings.
findings.check-command: kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l component=kube-apiserver -o jsonpath='{.items[0].spec.containers[0].command}' | grep authorization-mode

findings.title: The Kubernetes kubelet must not disable anonymous authentication
findings.implementation: RKE2 configures kubelet with anonymous authentication disabled by default (authentication.anonymous.enabled=false in kubelet config).

findings.title: The Kubernetes kubelet must enable explicit authorization
findings.implementation: RKE2 configures kubelet with Webhook authorization mode by default, delegating authorization decisions to the API server.
findings.check-command: ps aux | grep kubelet | grep authorization-mode

findings.title: The Kubernetes API Server must have the insecure port flag disabled
findings.implementation: RKE2 does not expose an insecure API server port. The --insecure-port=0 setting is applied by default.
findings.check-command: ps aux | grep kube-apiserver | grep insecure-port

findings.title: The Kubernetes API Server must have the insecure bind address not set
findings.implementation: RKE2 does not set an insecure bind address. The API server only listens on the secure port with TLS.
findings.check-command: ps aux | grep kube-apiserver | grep insecure-bind-address

findings.title: The Kubernetes kubelet must not enable the read-only port
findings.implementation: RKE2 disables the kubelet read-only port by default (--read-only-port=0).
findings.check-command: ps aux | grep kubelet | grep read-only-port

findings.title: The Kubernetes API Server must have audit logging enabled
findings.implementation: RKE2 enables audit logging by default with a comprehensive audit policy. Logs are forwarded to Loki via Alloy for centralized retention and analysis.

findings.title: The Kubernetes API Server must be set to audit log max age
findings.implementation: RKE2 sets audit log max age to 30 days by default. Long-term audit retention is handled by Loki with configurable retention policies (30-90 days).
findings.check-command: ps aux | grep kube-apiserver | grep audit-log-maxage

findings.title: The Kubernetes API Server must be set to audit log max backup
findings.implementation: RKE2 configures audit log max backup to retain multiple log files. Combined with Alloy log collection, no audit events are lost during rotation.
findings.check-command: ps aux | grep kube-apiserver | grep audit-log-maxbackup

findings.title: The Kubernetes API Server must be set to audit log max size
findings.implementation: RKE2 sets audit log max size to prevent disk exhaustion. Alloy collects logs in real-time, ensuring no data loss during rotation.
findings.check-command: ps aux | grep kube-apiserver | grep audit-log-maxsize

findings.title: The Kubernetes API Server must have an audit log path set
findings.implementation: RKE2 sets the audit log path to /var/lib/rancher/rke2/server/logs/audit.log by default.
findings.check-command: ps aux | grep kube-apiserver | grep audit-log-path

findings.title: The Kubernetes kubelet must protect kernel defaults
findings.implementation: RKE2 configures kubelet with --protect-kernel-defaults=true in the CIS hardened profile, ensuring kubelet does not modify kernel parameters.
findings.check-command: ps aux | grep kubelet | grep protect-kernel-defaults

findings.title: The Kubernetes kubelet static pod path must not enable static pods
findings.implementation: RKE2 uses its own static pod management for control plane components. Additional static pod paths are not configured.
findings.check-command: ps aux | grep kubelet | grep staticPodPath

findings.title: The Kubernetes API Server must have the streaming connection timeout set
findings.implementation: RKE2 sets streaming connection idle timeout to prevent resource exhaustion from abandoned connections.
findings.check-command: ps aux | grep kube-apiserver | grep streaming-connection-idle-timeout

findings.title: The Kubernetes API Server must have an HTTPS proxy configured
findings.implementation: RKE2 API server communicates with kubelets over HTTPS. The --kubelet-certificate-authority flag is set, ensuring certificate verification for kubelet connections.
findings.check-command: ps aux | grep kube-apiserver | grep kubelet-certificate-authority

findings.title: The Kubernetes cluster must use non-privileged host ports for user pods
findings.implementation: Kyverno disallow-host-ports ClusterPolicy restricts host port usage in tenant namespaces. All application services use ClusterIP. Istio gateway manages external port exposure.

findings.title: Secrets in Kubernetes must not be stored as environment variables
findings.implementation: External Secrets Operator (ESO) syncs secrets from OpenBao as Kubernetes Secret resources. Application Helm templates mount secrets as volumes, not environment variables, following security best practices.
findings.check-command: kubectl get pods -A -o json | jq '.items[].spec.containers[].env[]? | select(.valueFrom.secretKeyRef != null)' | head -10

findings.title: The Kubernetes cluster must use non-privileged containers
findings.implementation: Kyverno disallow-privileged ClusterPolicy prevents privileged containers in all tenant namespaces. Platform components with documented security exceptions (NeuVector DaemonSet) are explicitly exempted.

findings.title: Kubernetes must separate user functionality from system management functionality
findings.implementation: Platform components run in dedicated system namespaces (istio-system, monitoring, logging, kyverno, etc.). Tenant workloads run in separate namespaces. NetworkPolicies enforce isolation. RBAC prevents tenant access to system namespaces.

findings.title: The Kubernetes API Server must have Alpha APIs disabled
findings.implementation: RKE2 does not enable alpha APIs by default. The --runtime-config flag does not include any alpha API groups.
findings.check-command: ps aux | grep kube-apiserver | grep runtime-config

findings.title: The Kubernetes API Server must enable the PodSecurity admission controller
findings.implementation: Kyverno provides equivalent functionality to PodSecurity admission controller with more granular policy control. Kyverno ClusterPolicies enforce baseline and restricted Pod Security Standards.

findings.title: Kubernetes Secrets must be encrypted at rest
findings.implementation: RKE2 encrypts Kubernetes Secrets at rest by default using AES-CBC encryption. The encryption configuration is managed automatically by RKE2.

findings.title: The Kubernetes etcd must have peer-auto-tls set to false
findings.implementation: RKE2 embedded etcd uses explicitly generated peer certificates, not auto-generated TLS. peer-auto-tls is disabled.
findings.check-command: ps aux | grep etcd | grep peer-auto-tls

findings.title: The Kubernetes kubelet must not set readOnlyPort to 0
findings.implementation: RKE2 sets kubelet readOnlyPort to 0 (disabled) by default in the CIS hardened profile.
findings.check-command: ps aux | grep kubelet | grep read-only-port

findings.title: The Kubernetes kubelet must enable kernel protection
findings.implementation: RKE2 configures kubelet with --protect-kernel-defaults=true. SELinux enforcing mode provides additional kernel protection.
findings.check-command: ps aux | grep kubelet | grep protect-kernel-defaults

findings.title: The Kubernetes dashboard must not be enabled
findings.implementation: The default Kubernetes dashboard is not deployed. Platform monitoring is provided by Grafana with Keycloak SSO authentication.
findings.check-command: kubectl get deployment -n kubernetes-dashboard 2>/dev/null || echo 'Dashboard not deployed'

findings.title: The Kubernetes cluster must not use the default service account
findings.implementation: Kyverno disallow-default-namespace policy prevents deployments in the default namespace. Application Helm charts create dedicated ServiceAccounts per workload. automountServiceAccountToken is set to false by default.

findings.title: The Kubernetes kubelet must have the hostname-override set
findings.implementation: RKE2 configures kubelet hostname-override to match the node hostname for proper node identification.
findings.check-command: ps aux | grep kubelet | grep hostname-override

findings.title: The Kubernetes kubelet must not set event-qps to 0
findings.implementation: RKE2 configures kubelet event-qps to a non-zero value, ensuring event rate limiting is enabled to prevent denial of service.
findings.check-command: ps aux | grep kubelet | grep event-qps

findings.title: Kubernetes must remove old components after updated versions have been installed
findings.implementation: Flux CD manages component lifecycle via HelmRelease. Upgraded components automatically replace old versions. Harbor garbage collection removes old image layers. Velero backup schedules include retention policies.

findings.title: The Kubernetes API Server must be set to use only strong cryptographic ciphers
findings.implementation: RKE2 FIPS mode restricts the API server to FIPS-approved cipher suites only. TLS cipher suite configuration follows CIS benchmark recommendations.
findings.check-command: ps aux | grep kube-apiserver | grep tls-cipher-suites

findings.title: Kubernetes etcd must have a certificate for communication
findings.implementation: RKE2 auto-generates etcd server and client certificates during bootstrap. All etcd communication is authenticated and encrypted via mTLS.

findings.title: Kubernetes etcd must have the SSL Certificate Authority set
findings.implementation: RKE2 generates a dedicated CA for etcd and configures all etcd components to use it for certificate verification.

findings.title: Kubernetes etcd must have a key file for secure communication
findings.implementation: RKE2 generates etcd server and peer key files during bootstrap for secure mTLS communication.

findings.title: Kubernetes etcd must have peer-cert-file set for secure communication
findings.implementation: RKE2 configures etcd with peer certificate files for secure peer-to-peer communication in multi-node etcd clusters.
findings.check-command: ps aux | grep etcd | grep peer-cert-file

findings.title: Kubernetes must use an approved container registry
findings.implementation: Kyverno restrict-image-registries ClusterPolicy only allows images from harbor.sre.internal. All other registry sources are denied at admission time.

findings.title: Kubernetes must verify image signatures before deployment
findings.implementation: Kyverno verify-image-signatures ClusterPolicy enforces Cosign signature verification on all pod images at admission time. Only images signed with the approved key can be deployed.

findings.title: Kubernetes must have a Network Policy for each namespace
findings.implementation: Kyverno require-network-policies ClusterPolicy ensures every namespace has at least a default-deny NetworkPolicy. All tenant namespaces include default-deny.yaml with explicit allow rules for required traffic.

findings.title: Kubernetes must have a Pod Security Policy
findings.implementation: Pod Security Standards are enforced via Kyverno (replacing deprecated PodSecurityPolicy). Baseline and restricted profiles are implemented as ClusterPolicies.

findings.title: Kubernetes must limit the number of concurrent connections
findings.implementation: RKE2 API server has --max-requests-inflight and --max-mutating-requests-inflight configured. Istio provides circuit breaking and rate limiting for mesh traffic.
findings.check-command: ps aux | grep kube-apiserver | grep max-requests-inflight

findings.title: Kubernetes must have a resource quota for each namespace
findings.implementation: All tenant namespaces include ResourceQuota and LimitRange resources managed via GitOps. This prevents resource exhaustion and ensures fair sharing across tenants.

findings.title: Kubernetes must use resource limits for workloads
findings.implementation: Kyverno require-resource-limits ClusterPolicy enforces that all pods specify CPU and memory resource requests and limits. Application Helm chart templates include default resource specifications.

findings.title: Kubernetes must contain the latest updates
findings.implementation: RKE2 version is pinned and tracked in Git. Upgrades are performed via Ansible playbook (upgrade-rke2.yml) with rolling update strategy. Flux tracks all platform component versions via HelmRelease version pins.

findings.title: The Kubernetes component etcd must be owned by the etcd service account
findings.implementation: RKE2 manages etcd as an embedded component with proper ownership and permissions set during bootstrap.

findings.title: The Kubernetes kubelet config must have file permissions set to 644 or more restrictive
findings.implementation: RKE2 sets kubelet config file permissions to 0600 during installation. The rke2-hardening Ansible role verifies and enforces these permissions.

findings.title: The Kubernetes kubelet config must be owned by root
findings.implementation: RKE2 sets kubelet config ownership to root:root during installation.

findings.title: The Kubernetes component PKI must be owned by root
findings.implementation: RKE2 PKI directories and files are owned by root. The rke2-hardening Ansible role verifies ownership.

findings.title: The Kubernetes component PKI must have file permissions set to 644 or more restrictive
findings.implementation: RKE2 sets PKI file permissions to 0600 for key files and 0644 for certificate files. The rke2-hardening Ansible role verifies and enforces these permissions.

findings.title: The Kubernetes API Server must have the ValidatingAdmissionWebhook enabled
findings.implementation: Kyverno operates as a ValidatingAdmissionWebhook. RKE2 enables the ValidatingAdmissionWebhook admission plugin by default.

findings.title: The Kubernetes API Server must have the MutatingAdmissionWebhook enabled
findings.implementation: Kyverno operates as a MutatingAdmissionWebhook for auto-injecting security defaults. Istio uses a MutatingAdmissionWebhook for sidecar injection. RKE2 enables the MutatingAdmissionWebhook admission plugin by default.

findings.title: The Kubernetes cluster must implement DOD-approved encryption
findings.implementation: RKE2 FIPS mode uses GoBoring Go compiler with FIPS 140-2 validated cryptographic modules. Rocky Linux 9 FIPS crypto policy restricts OS-level cryptography to FIPS-approved algorithms. All TLS connections use TLS 1.2+ with FIPS-approved cipher suites.

findings.title: Kubernetes must separate user functionality
findings.implementation: User workloads run in dedicated tenant namespaces (team-alpha, team-beta, etc.) with ResourceQuotas, LimitRanges, and NetworkPolicies. System services run in protected platform namespaces. RBAC enforces separation.

findings.title: The Kubernetes API Server must configure timeouts
findings.implementation: RKE2 configures API server request timeout (--request-timeout) by default. Streaming connection idle timeout prevents resource leaks from abandoned connections.
findings.check-command: ps aux | grep kube-apiserver | grep request-timeout

findings.title: The Kubernetes API server must not enable the ServiceAccount admission controller plugin for token validation
findings.implementation: RKE2 manages ServiceAccount admission controller configuration. Requires verification in the deployed environment to confirm exact admission controller ordering.
findings.check-command: ps aux | grep kube-apiserver | grep enable-admission-plugins

findings.title: The Kubernetes API server must not bind to non-loopback insecure addresses
findings.implementation: RKE2 does not configure an insecure bind address. The API server only listens on the secure port with TLS.

findings.title: Kubernetes must have a CNI plugin installed
findings.implementation: RKE2 ships with Canal (Calico + Flannel) as the default CNI plugin, providing NetworkPolicy enforcement. Canal is automatically deployed during RKE2 installation.

findings.title: Kubernetes must enable audit logging
findings.implementation: RKE2 enables comprehensive audit logging by default. Audit logs are collected by Alloy and forwarded to Loki for centralized analysis and long-term retention.

findings.title: Kubernetes must be configured to use a secure container runtime
findings.implementation: RKE2 uses containerd as the container runtime (not Docker). containerd provides a minimal, secure runtime with support for seccomp, AppArmor, and SELinux.

findings.title: The Kubernetes cluster must use RBAC for access control
findings.implementation: RKE2 enables RBAC as the authorization mode by default. All tenant namespaces have scoped Roles and RoleBindings mapped to Keycloak groups.
findings.check-command: ps aux | grep kube-apiserver | grep authorization-mode

findings.title: Kubernetes service accounts must be properly scoped
findings.implementation: Application Helm charts create dedicated ServiceAccounts per workload. automountServiceAccountToken set to false. Requires verification that all deployed workloads use scoped ServiceAccounts.
findings.check-command: kubectl get serviceaccounts -A --no-headers | grep -v default | wc -l

findings.title: Kubernetes must not use deprecated API versions
findings.implementation: All platform manifests use current API versions (apps/v1, v1, networking.k8s.io/v1). Flux validates API versions during reconciliation. CI pipeline checks for deprecated API usage.

findings.title: Kubernetes must implement image provenance
findings.implementation: Complete image provenance chain: (1) Images built in CI with Trivy scanning, (2) Signed with Cosign, (3) SBOM generated with Syft, (4) Stored in Harbor, (5) Kyverno verifies signatures at admission, (6) Kyverno restricts registries to Harbor only.

findings.title: Kubernetes must use a container runtime with security features
findings.implementation: RKE2 uses containerd with seccomp RuntimeDefault profile enforced by Kyverno. SELinux enforcing mode on all nodes. AppArmor profiles available on compatible distributions.

findings.title: Kubernetes must enable TLS for all network traffic
findings.implementation: All cluster communication encrypted: Istio mTLS STRICT for pod-to-pod, RKE2 mTLS for control plane, cert-manager TLS for ingress, SSH with FIPS-approved ciphers for node access.
findings.check-command: kubectl get peerauthentication -A -o yaml | grep mtls

findings.title: Kubernetes must perform validation of service connections
findings.implementation: Istio AuthorizationPolicies validate service connections based on SPIFFE identity, namespace, and request attributes. Kyverno validates pod configurations at admission. NeuVector validates network connections against learned baselines.

findings.title: The Kubernetes cluster must have a backup and recovery plan
findings.implementation: Velero provides automated cluster backup with daily, weekly, and monthly schedules. Restore testing validates backup integrity. Disaster recovery runbooks document the recovery procedure. Requires verification of backup schedule execution in the deployed environment.

findings.title: Kubernetes must monitor pod health
findings.implementation: Application Helm chart templates include liveness and readiness probes by default. Prometheus monitors pod health via kube-state-metrics. Kyverno require-probes policy can enforce probe requirements. Requires verification of probe configuration on deployed workloads.
findings.check-command: kubectl get pods -A -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.spec.containers[].livenessProbe == null) | .metadata.name'
